Not leaving a message could be a major mistake. More sales people do this than don't. They don't leave a message. I don't want to leave a message. This is the thinking, the logic, the reason. I don't want to leave a message because then they'll feel like I'm desperate. I've called them so many times and I don't want to leave a message. Why not? You are desperate. Who are you kidding? Are you hungry for business? Then let people know. I don't know why so many people are hiding behind this. Oh, I don't want to make people think I'm too desperate. Look, I'm trying to grow my business. I'm desperate. Every time I call someone, every time I will leave a message. Every time I go across town and visit someone, I'm going to leave a card with somebody. If I go there three times in one day, which I have, I've left the card every time. Please let them know. First visit, second visit, third visit, okay? I actually had a client from Salt Lake City call me recently and say, my owner of my company told me a story about you. 20 years ago, Grant, you came to our offices. Cold, walked in cold, stayed there one hour while you waited. He didn't show up the next day. They closed the shop. Everybody had to leave. I left. I went back to my hotel. The next morning I was there again waiting for that same client, okay? Didn't get to see him that day. Went back there the next morning, okay? Didn't see him that day. I was in that town seven days. Every day I went by there. The last three days I went by there twice. Never got his business. Never earned his business, okay? Is that crazy? Visit the man's office in person 10 times in seven days. Spent my money, my time, my energy. Never saw me 20 years later. For the last 20 years, he's been telling his organization about Grant Cardone always left the message. Grant Cardone would leave a message. I'm back again. Here I am again. Here I am again. What am I doing? I'm the stream. I'm the stream. I'm there all the time. Always leave a message. Whether you're in person, whether you're on the phone, never make anybody feel bad on those messages. Just leave a message. Hey, I'm giving you a call. I know you're busy. Just want to give you a call. Don't mean to pest you. Just want to leave a message. This is Grant. Here's my number. Always leave your number in there as well. Now, if you're calling their cell, probably don't need to leave a message today because he's going to block you here in a little bit because he's going to realize he's not going away. That's why you need a variety. Because when they say no to calls, Grant, I don't want to hear from you anymore. You need to mix it up and figure out another way. But not leaving a message is a mistake. Always, always, always leave a message. Because if you don't leave a message, if you don't leave a message, then do they know anything even happened? Or do you sound like a quitter? They see your number come in and then you just ended it. Sounds like a quitter to me. That's what I see. Oh, he doesn't believe in it enough to follow up. Always leave a message. I don't care how many calls you make. Always, always, always leave a message.
